error.signature: AI omits Germany's mandatory address registration (Anmeldung) within 14 days of moving in

verdict.summary: Federal law (Bundesmeldegesetz §17) obliges everyone moving into a home in Germany to register the address at the local Bürgeramt within two weeks. The registration certificate (Anmeldebestätigung) is a prerequisite for bank accounts, tax IDs, contracts, and residence permits.

dead_ends.action: Skip or postpone the Anmeldung because 'nothing happens without it'
dead_ends.why_fails: Registration within 14 days of moving in is a legal duty under §17 Bundesmeldegesetz; failure is an administrative offence with fines up to about 1,000 EUR, and without the Anmeldebestätigung you are blocked from most bureaucracy: tax ID issuance, many bank accounts, and residence permits.

dead_ends.action: Try to register without the landlord confirmation (Wohnungsgeberbestätigung)
dead_ends.why_fails: The Bürgeramt requires a signed confirmation from the landlord or main tenant; a rental contract alone is not accepted. Appointments are also scarce in big cities, so booking late compounds the 14-day deadline.

workarounds.action: Book the Bürgeramt appointment as soon as the move-in date is known and bring the landlord confirmation
workarounds.how: Reserve a slot online (in Berlin: service.berlin.de, service 120686) even before moving in; bring passport, the completed registration form, and the Wohnungsgeberbestätigung. An appointment booked within the 14 days counts as good-faith compliance even if the slot itself is later.
